description: Microblog (Twitter/X-like) legacy aggregate targets: PUBLISHED AGGREGATE statistics only (no individual-level data). Research approximations with citations; tolerances are deliberately wide where the literature disagrees. Citation research pass 2026-07-11: named-paper identifiers were verified against arXiv/dblp/official pages (see per-target source_status); numeric values were NOT independently re-verified, and the full metadata schema (population, date range, source hash, tolerance rationale) remains incomplete, so every target stays kind=unsupported and comparison gates stay closed.

degree_tail_exponent.source: Follower-degree power-law exponent ~2.3-2.4 (Kwak, Lee, Park & Moon 2010, 'What is Twitter, a Social Network or a News Media?', WWW; Myers et al. 2014)
degree_tail_exponent.verification_note: Kwak et al. 2010 (WWW'10) DOI verified via the authors' official dataset page on the retrieval date. HONESTY NOTE: the paper's own abstract reports a NON-power-law follower distribution; the ~2.3 exponent is a partial-range fit from the paper body, not re-verified from an open source in this pass. Myers et al. 2014 (WWW'14 Companion pp.493-498, DOI 10.1145/2567948.2576939, open PDF at https://archives.iw3c2.org/www2014/proceedings/companion/p493.pdf) verified as analyzing the follow-graph degree distributions.
degree_tail_exponent.source_title: What is Twitter, a Social Network or a News Media?
degree_tail_exponent.tolerance_rationale: scenario_threshold_not_derived: no statistical derivation exists for this tolerance; it is a scenario knob, not a confidence bound
degree_tail_exponent.applicability_limits: aggregate-fit diagnostics only; the numeric value is unverified against any source (null fields above are 'not established', not 'not applicable')
valid_uses: input to the aggregate-fit implausibility diagnostic
synthetic scenario anchoring under stated assumptions

clustering.source: Low average clustering ~0.1 for the follower graph (Kwak et al. 2010)
clustering.verification_note: Kwak et al. 2010 identifier verified (see degree_tail_exponent note); the ~0.1 clustering figure was not re-verified against the paper body in this pass.
clustering.source_title: What is Twitter, a Social Network or a News Media?
